title: काली माता आरती
devanagariText:
॥ श्री काली माता आरती ॥

जय काली माता, मैया जय काली माता।
तुम हो जगत जननी, सबकी सुखदाता॥

जय काली माता॥

श्यामा वरन विकराला, रूप अति भारी।
मुण्डमाल गल सोहे, लाल जिह्वा धारी॥

जय काली माता॥

खड्ग खप्पर हाथन, रुण्ड मुण्ड साजे।
शिव शरीर पर चरणा, विकट छवि राजे॥

जय काली माता॥

रक्तबीज संहारा, चामुण्डा रूपा।
चण्ड मुण्ड विनाशा, महिषासुर भूपा॥

जय काली माता॥

शुम्भ निशुम्भ विदारा, दैत्य संहारे।
भक्त जनन की रक्षा, करो माँ प्यारे॥

जय काली माता॥

कालीघाट विराजो, दक्षिणेश्वर वासी।
तारापीठ में माता, सबकी सुखरासी॥

जय काली माता॥

दीपावली काली पूजा, बंगाल में होवे।
रामकृष्ण परमहंस, माँ दर्शन जोवे॥

जय काली माता॥

श्री काली माता की, आरती जो गावे।
कहत सदा सुखदायी, भय सब मिट जावे॥

जय काली माता॥

॥ इति श्री काली माता आरती सम्पूर्ण ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Kali Mata Aarti ||

Jai Kali Mata, Maiya Jai Kali Mata.
Tum Ho Jagat Janani, Sabki Sukhdata.

Jai Kali Mata.

Shyama Varan Vikrala, Roop Ati Bhari.
Mundmal Gal Sohe, Lal Jihva Dhari.

Jai Kali Mata.

Khadg Khappar Hathan, Rund Mund Saje.
Shiv Sharir Par Charna, Vikat Chhavi Raje.

Jai Kali Mata.

Raktabeej Sanhara, Chamunda Roopa.
Chand Mund Vinasha, Mahishasur Bhoopa.

Jai Kali Mata.

Shumbh Nishumbh Vidara, Daitya Sanhare.
Bhakt Janan Ki Raksha, Karo Maa Pyare.

Jai Kali Mata.

Kalighat Virajo, Dakshineshwar Vasi.
Tarapeeth Mein Mata, Sabki Sukhrasi.

Jai Kali Mata.

Deepawali Kali Puja, Bangal Mein Hove.
Ramkrishna Paramhans, Maa Darshan Jove.

Jai Kali Mata.

Shri Kali Mata Ki, Aarti Jo Gave.
Kahat Sada Sukhdayi, Bhay Sab Mit Jave.

Jai Kali Mata.

|| Iti Shri Kali Mata Aarti Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
जय काली माता! जगत जननी, सबकी सुखदाता! श्यामा वर्ण विकराल रूप, मुण्डमाल गले में शोभित, लाल जिह्वा धारिणी।

हाथों में खड्ग और खप्पर, रुण्ड मुण्ड सजे हुए। शिव के शरीर पर चरण रखे, विकट छवि राजे। रक्तबीज का संहार किया, चामुण्डा रूप धरा, चण्ड-मुण्ड का विनाश किया।
benefits: Traditionally recited for removal of all fears, evil forces, and negative energies
Provides fierce protection to devotees in times of crisis
Essential aarti for Kali Puja on Deepawali night
Invokes the most powerful form of the Divine Mother
description: Kali Aarti — काली माता आरती — is a traditional worship song (aarti) offered to Goddess Kali. This revered text originates from Traditional Aarti Sangrah. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to destroy all fears, evil forces, and negative energies as well as provide fierce protection to devotees in times of crisis. Additionally, it is known to invoke the most powerful form of the Divine Mother. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Kali Puja, Diwali, and Navratri. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 4 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Kali Aarti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Kali Mata Aarti in Hindi with meaning and transliteration. Jai Kali Mata - powerful aarti for Kali Puja and protection from evil.
relatedSearches: Kali Aarti lyrics in Hindi and Bengali
Ambe tu hai jagadambe kali lyrics
Difference between Durga and Kali aarti

seoFaqs.question: When is Kali Aarti traditionally performed?
seoFaqs.answer: Kali Aarti is performed daily in Kali temples, with morning, midday, evening and late-night sessions in major shrines such as Dakshineswar and Kalighat in Kolkata. The most important occasion in the year is Kali Puja, the new moon night of the Kartika month (Kartik amavasya), which usually coincides with Diwali night in north India but is observed in Bengal and parts of Odisha and Assam as a distinct festival in its own right. Kali Aarti is also performed on every amavasya through the year, on Tuesdays and Saturdays which are the days of Devi worship, during the nine nights of Navaratri, and on Kali Chaudas, the fourteenth night of Kartika krishna paksha.

seoFaqs.question: How is Kali Puja different from the wider Durga Puja?
seoFaqs.answer: Durga Puja, observed in Ashvin shukla paksha, celebrates Durga's victory over Mahishasura and is a daytime, public festival lasting nine nights, with elaborately decorated pandals and family gatherings. Kali Puja is observed about three weeks later on Kartika amavasya night, is overwhelmingly nocturnal, and has a more tantric and intimate character, often performed in temples or homes rather than public pandals. Where Durga is worshipped as the warrior-mother in radiant form, Kali is worshipped in her dark, ferocious aspect as the destroyer of ego and time. Both deities are understood in Bengali Shakta tradition as the same Adyashakti in different moods.

seoFaqs.question: What is the role of Shyama Sangeet in Kali worship?
seoFaqs.answer: Shyama Sangeet is a genre of Bengali devotional songs addressed to Kali (Shyama, the dark one) that flourished from the eighteenth century onward. The two great composers are Ramprasad Sen and Kamalakanta Bhattacharya, and their songs combine intense personal devotion, philosophical reflection on time and death, and an unmistakable Bengali emotional vocabulary in which the goddess is addressed as ma (mother). Kali Aarti in Bengali tradition is often interleaved with these songs, sung either by the priest or by the assembled devotees. The combination of formal aarti and Shyama Sangeet creates an atmosphere that is at once reverential and movingly personal.

seoFaqs.question: What does the imagery of Kali in the aarti symbolise?
seoFaqs.answer: The aarti praises Kali in her classical iconography: dark or blue-black complexion, lolling tongue, garland of severed heads, skirt of severed arms, four arms holding a sword and a severed head while two are raised in abhaya and varada mudras, standing on the supine body of Shiva. Each element carries meaning: the colour represents the void from which all forms emerge, the heads and arms represent the dissolution of ego and karma, the sword cuts attachment, and her stance on Shiva represents the activation of pure consciousness by primordial energy. The aarti, by repeatedly invoking these images, makes the worshipper meditate on the impermanence of forms and the eternity of the Mother.

seoFaqs.question: Can Kali Aarti be sung outside Bengali tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. While the Kali Puja festival is most elaborate in Bengal, Kali worship is widespread across India: at Kamakhya in Assam, at the Bhadrakali temples of Kerala and Andhra, at Dakshina Kali shrines in Tamil Nadu, and at the many Mahakali temples of Maharashtra and Gujarat. The Hindi aarti Ambe Tu Hai Jagadambe Kali, sung across north India, treats Kali as one face of the universal Mother. Devotees outside Bengal commonly sing this Hindi aarti during Navaratri and on Tuesdays, while those drawn to the Bengali Shakta tradition learn Bengali aartis and Shyama Sangeet to deepen their connection to the more specifically tantric forms of Kali.

seoFaqs.question: Are there ethical or behavioural recommendations connected with Kali worship?
seoFaqs.answer: Tantric tradition emphasises that Kali, despite her fierce iconography, is the most compassionate Mother to those who approach her with sincerity, and that her sword cuts only ego, not the devotee. Standard recommendations connected with regular Kali Aarti include observing vegetarian or sattvic food on Tuesdays and Saturdays, practising sexual restraint and truthful speech, avoiding harm to women and children since she is the protector of the weak, and serving the elderly and the sick. The wider point made by Shakta teachers is that Kali Puja without inner ethical work becomes superstition, while Kali Puja paired with right conduct becomes a transformative sadhana that destroys inner enemies far more reliably than outer ones.
